title_slide: Waking Up to What We've Built

slides.heading: Waking Up to What We've Built
bullet_points: And the world that punishes it
slides.speaker_note: Let the title sit — don't rush into explanation, the stillness is part of the tone.

slides.heading: The Moment It Came From
bullet_points: Watching someone get torn apart for being real
Watching the hollow ones get praised
slides.speaker_note: Frame the poem's origin before the poem itself; it changes how the words land.

slides.heading: The Poem
bullet_points: Praising the false gods / demeaning deserving humans
Only to make themselves happy — twice
slides.speaker_note: Read the poem slowly; the repeated ending is the structural center, don't flatten it.

slides.heading: The Mask Paradox
bullet_points: The masked aren't confident — they're defended
The mask needs the real face to disappear
slides.speaker_note: This is the core structural insight — aggression here isn't random, it's protective.

slides.heading: What This Does to Creators
bullet_points: Honest work often underperforms polished performance
The costume grows faster than the body of work
Every creator must choose which one to build
slides.speaker_note: Make it specific and personal here — this is where the essay earns the poem.

slides.heading: The Waking Up
bullet_points: The poem doesn't offer a fix
slides.speaker_note: Close without a motivational pivot — let the clarity stand alone.
